title: School Readiness Programs - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Education > School Readiness Programs 71 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Hispanic/Latino Community Refugees/Entrants/Asylees ALBINA HEAD START HEAD START Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Head Start services promote school readiness by enhancing the social and cognitive development of children in a classroom setting and by providing educational, health, nutritional, social, and other services to children and families. Parental engagement is emphasized. Services for parents include goal setting and support, parenting classes, and opportunities for parents to network. 3417 NE 7th Avenue Portland, OR 97212 Eligibility: Families with children ages 3-5. Families with incomes at or below 100% FPL are eligible. Children in foster care, homeless children, and children from families receiving public assistance (Temporary Assistance for Needy Families, Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) or Supplemental Security Income) are eligible regardless of income. Families with higher incomes and specific needs may also be eligible, but families with higher needs are prioritized. Encourages parent engagement through goal setting, parenting classes, and networking opportunities. 108 W B Street Rainier, OR 97048 Eligibility: Families with children ages 3–5. Families with incomes at or below 100% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL) are eligible. Families with higher incomes may also qualify if specific needs are present, though those with higher needs are given priority. Parental engagement is emphasized. Services for parents include goal setting and support, parenting classes and opportunities for parents to network. Head Start is also offered at Coffee Creek Correctional Facility through a partnership with the Oregon Department of Corrections. Teachers help incarcerated mothers bond with their children, learn parenting skills, and prepare for parenting after being released. 17933 NW Evergreen Place Suite 315 Portland, OR 97229 Eligibility: Families with children ages 3-5. Families with higher incomes and specific needs may also be eligible, but families with higher needs are prioritized. Coffee Creek Program: Head Start staff collaborate with the Department of Corrections in determining incarcerated mothers' eligibility to participate. Head Start services foster school readiness by nurturing children’s social and cognitive development in a classroom environment. In addition, a comprehensive array of services, including educational, health, nutritional, and social support, benefits both children and families. Parental engagement is a key focus, with services that include goal-setting support, parenting classes, and opportunities for parents to connect and network. 201 SE Swan Avenue Siletz, OR 97380 Eligibility: Families with children ages 3-5. Preference for enrollment to children who are Native American. Tribal families are given first priority for enrollment followed by Native Americans of other Tribes and non-native children. Hours: September-May, Monday-Thursday 7:40am-3:45 pm Email (541) 756-0904 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DEPARTMENT OF EARLY LEARNING AND CARE DEPARTMENT OF EARLY LEARNING AND CARE CHILD CARE OREGON PRENATAL TO KINDERGARTEN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Fosters school readiness in an inclusive classroom setting by supporting the cognitive, social, emotional, and physical development of children. Supports families and children with health services, home visits, family engagement, and parent leadership skills. Website Eligibility: Pregnant parents or children ages 0-5. Families income must be equal to or below 100% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL) or is categorically eligible. Categorical eligibility includes SNAP, TANF, foster families, and families experiencing homelessness. Families experiencing domestic violence, parental incarceration, substance use disorder, or child welfare involvement may also qualify based on demonstrated need. Hours: Varies Email (800) 699-9075 Visit Website More Details DOUGLAS EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT SOUTH-CENTRAL EARLY LEARNING HUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Works to develop a coordinated, effective system of early learning services that equips young children with needed skills for success in school and life. Also helps connect parents to local resources and qualifies families for Preschool Promise. 1409 NE Diamond Lake Boulevard Suite 110 Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-4:30pm Email (541) 957-4807 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EARLY LEARNING HUB OF CENTRAL OREGON EARLY LEARNING HUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects families with leaders in healthcare, education, social services, early childhood, and business to develop strategies for preparing children to be successful achievers. 2362 Glacier Place Redmond, OR 97756 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 693-5769 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EARLY LEARNING HUB OF CENTRAL OREGON UPSTART Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF An online kindergarten readiness program. Offered in-home 20 minutes per day, 5 days per week. Parent coaching and support, Chromebook, and connections to school readiness resources included. Internet support provided if needed. 2362 Glacier Place Redmond, OR 97756 Eligibility: Child must be entering kindergarten within the next year Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 693-5769 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EARLY LEARNING HUB OF CLACKAMAS COUNTY CLACKAMAS EARLY LEARNING HUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A network of providers who work to enhance the ability of parents and caregivers to stimulate, protect, and nurture children. In order to lower the incidences of child abuse and neglect, increase effective parenting, and improve family health and well-being via parent education trainings, strengthening linkages among providers, and improving access to support for all families. Coordinated enrollment into subsidized preschool, including Head Start and Preschool Promise. Serves as the Coordinate Early Learning Hub for the county. 821 Main Street Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-8 Families at or below 200% of the Federal Poverty Level for subsidized preschool enrollment Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm Email (971) 288-8264 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EARLY LEARNING HUB OF LINN, BENTON, AND LINCOLN COUNTIES EARLY LEARNING HUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects families to partnering agencies to ensure that children are ready for kindergarten. 6500 Pacific Boulevard SW LM-132 Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (541) 917-4922 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EARLY LEARNING MULTNOMAH MULTNOMAH EARLY LEARNING HUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Holds community meetings to address issues in the early childhood system and school readiness. 619 SW 11th Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Visit website for meeting times Email (503) 226-9309 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EARLY LEARNING WASHINGTON COUNTY EARLY LEARNING HUB OF WASHINGTON COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Early Learning Washington County (ELWC) is a partnership that brings diverse organizations, parents, and communities together to support families in raising strong, healthy children. 155 N First Street Hillsboro, OR 97124 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 846-4918 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON UNIVERSITY HEAD START Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Head Start services promotes healthy families and communities by enhancing the social and cognitive development of children in a classroom setting and by providing eduactional, health, nutritional, social, and other services to children and families. Parental engagement is emphasized. Services for parents include goal setting and support, parenting classes and opportunities for parents to network. Head start sites are located in Baker City, Elgin, La Grand and Union. Early Head Start classroom and home visiting services are also available for families with children 0-3 years old. One University Boulevard La Grande, OR 978502807 Eligibility: Families with children ages 3-5. Families with incomes at or below 100% FPL are eligible. Children in foster care, homeless children, and children from families receiving public assistance (Temporary Assistance for Needy Families (TANF), SNAP, or Supplemental Security Income (SSI)) are eligible regardless of income. Families with higher incomes and specific needs may also be eligible, but families with higher needs are prioritized. Hours: Varies by site (541) 786-5535 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EUGENE FAMILY YMCA COMMUNITY RECREATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of recreation opportunities, including an aquatic center, gym, group exercise classes, preschool, afterschool childcare, family fun events, and youth and adult sports. Also provides teen youth clubs and drop-in events. The Virtual Y Hub provides on-demand videos for fitness, yoga, meditation, and more that people can watch at home. 600 E 24th Avenue Eugene, OR 97405 Eligibility: Unrestricted, some programs require YMCA membership Hours: Varies by site Email (541) 686-9622 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FOUR RIVERS EARLY LEARNING AND PARENTING EARLY LEARNING HUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers collaborative programs and systems for kindergarten readiness, as well as community events, children's playgroups, and classes for parents and guardians of young children. 400 E Scenic Drive Suite 207 The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Ages 0-5 children and their parent or guardian Hours: Varies Email (541) 993-2396 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FRIENDLY HOUSE CHILDREN'S PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides after-school enrichment program with activities such as art, science, homework help, sports, field trips, and playtime. Children have the opportunity to participate in weekly clubs like photography, theater, gardening, and art. Day camps are available during school breaks. 1737 NW 26th Avenue Portland, OR 97210 Eligibility: After-school program: K-5 grade students of Chapman Elementary Summer Camp: 1-6 grade students Winter Camp: K-5 grade students Spring Break Camp: K-5 grade students Hours: After-school program: Monday-Friday 7am-7:50am and 2:30pm-6pm Summer Camp: 9am-4pm; optional before care (7am-9am) and aftercare (4pm-6pm) Winter Camp: 7:30am-6pm Spring Break Camp: 7:30am-6pm Email (503) 228-4391 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GLADSTONE CENTER FOR CHILDREN AND FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides parenting materials, family events, and a free lending library. It also hosts a variety of classes and groups offered by the agencies within the center. The agencies it houses include the Gladstone Health & Wellness Center, pediatric health, emotional and dental services. Family Stepping Stones, Relief Nursery, Head Start and Early Head Start, Northwest Family services navigators, Clackamas Education Service District EI/ECSE program, LifeWorks Northwest family counseling, WIC, SMART, Clackamas Community College GED program, and the Gladstone School District kindergarten program. 18905 Portland Avenue Gladstone, OR 97027 Eligibility: Households with children from birth through kindergarten Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-4pm, also evening hours vary Email (503) 496-3939 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GRANTS PASS SCHOOL DISTRICT 7 READY FOR KINDERGARTEN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Teaches parents and guardians important skills that children should acquire before starting school.
